>

**输出：**`[[1,1,2],[1,2,2],[2,2,2]]`

每个时间步的网格如下：

#include Array
#include Hash

def solve(n : Int64, m : Int64, sources : Array(Array(Int64))) : Array(Array(Int64))
    # Convert N and M to Int for array indexing, safe since N*M <= 10^5
    n_int = n.to_i
    m_int = m.to_i
    
    # Grid: N x M, initialized to 0_i64
    grid = Array(Array(0_i64), n_int) { Array(0_i64, m_int) }
    
    # Active Cells: Stores coordinates [r, c] of cells that are currently colored
    active_cells = Array(Array(Int64))
    
    # Process sources
    sources.each do |source|
        r = source[0]
        c = source[1]
        color = source[2]
        
        grid[r.to_i][c.to_i] = color
        active_cells.push([r, c])
    end
    
    # Directions: Up, Down, Left, Right
    directions = [[-1, 0], [1, 0], [0, -1], [0, 1]]
    
    # Simulation Loop
    loop do
        # Next_Updates: Map (r, c) -> Max Color reaching it in this step
        # Using Array(Int64) as key for coordinates
        next_updates = Hash.new(Array(Int64) => Int64)
        
        # Iterate over all currently colored cells
        active_cells.each do |cell|
            r = cell[0]
            c = cell[1]
            current_color = grid[r.to_i][c.to_i]
            
            # Check neighbors
            directions.each do |dr, dc|
                r_prime = r + dr
                c_prime = c + dc
                
                # Check bounds
                if r_prime >= 0 && r_prime < n && c_prime >= 0 && c_prime < m
                    r_prime_int = r_prime.to_i
                    c_prime_int = c_prime.to_i
                    
                    # Check if uncolored
                    if grid[r_prime_int][c_prime_int] == 0_i64
                        
                        neighbor_key = [r_prime, c_prime]
                        
                        # Update next_updates: take the maximum color
                        current_max_color = next_updates[neighbor_key]
                        if current_color > current_max_color
                            next_updates[neighbor_key] = current_color
                        end
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        
        # Check termination
        if next_updates.empty?
            break
        end
        
        # Apply updates
        new_cells = Array(Array(Int64))
        
        next_updates.each do |key, new_color|
            r = key[0]
            c = key[1]
            r_int = r.to_i
            c_int = c.to_i
            
            # Only color if it hasn't been colored yet in this step
            if grid[r_int][c_int] == 0_i64
                grid[r_int][c_int] = new_color
                new_cells.push([r, c])
            end
        end
        
        # Update active_cells for the next iteration
        active_cells.push(*new_cells)
    end
    
    return grid
end

#include Hash

    m_int = m.to_i
    
    # 1. Initialization
    
    # Grid: N x M, initialized to 0
    grid = Array(Array(0_i64), n_int) { Array(0_i64, m_int) }
    
    # Active Cells: Stores coordinates [r, c] of cells that are currently colored
    active_cells = Array(Array(Int64))
    
    # Process sources
    sources.each do |source|
        r = source[0]
        c = source[1]
        color = source[2]
        
        grid[r.to_i][c.to_i] = color
        active_cells.push([r, c])
    end
    
    # Directions: Up, Down, Left, Right
    directions = [[-1, 0], [1, 0], [0, -1], [0, 1]]
    
    # 2. Simulation Loop
    
    loop do
        # Next_Updates: Map (r, c) -> Max Color reaching it in this step
        # Key structure: [r, c] array of Int64
        next_updates = Hash.new(Array(Int64) => Int64)
        
        # Step 2.b & 2.c: Iterate over all currently colored cells
        active_cells.each do |cell|
            r = cell[0]
            c = cell[1]
            current_color = grid[r.to_i][c.to_i]
            
            # Step 2.d: Check neighbors
            directions.each do |dr, dc|
                r_prime = r + dr
                c_prime = c + dc
                
                # Check bounds
                if r_prime >= 0 && r_prime < n && c_prime >= 0 && c_prime < m
                    r_prime_int = r_prime.to_i
                    c_prime_int = c_prime.to_i

                    if grid[r_prime_int][c_prime_int] == 0_i64
                        
                        neighbor_key = [r_prime, c_prime]
                        
                        # Update next_updates: take the maximum color
                        current_max_color = next_updates[neighbor_key]
                        if current_color > current_max_color
                            next_updates[neighbor_key] = current_color
                        end
                    end
                end
            end
        end
        
        # Step 2.e: Check termination
        if next_updates.empty?
            break
        end
        
        # Step 2.f: Apply updates
        new_cells = Array(Array(Int64))
        
        next_updates.each do |key, new_color|
            r = key[0]
            c = key[1]
            r_int = r.to_i
            c_int = c.to_i

            # Since we iterate over next_updates which summarizes the MAX color for this step,
            # we only need to check if it hasn't been colored by an *earlier* source in this step.
            # Given how we build next_updates, this check should ideally always pass if it was 0 initially.
            if grid[r_int][c_int] == 0_i64

                new_cells.push([r, c])
            end
        end

    end
    
    return grid
end
